38: **Spain** arrive at this stage as one of the slickest, most possession-dominant sides in the draw, and **Saudi Arabia** will need a tactical masterclass to contain them. The Saudis showed in recent tournaments they can shock the world, but La Roja's depth and structural quality make them heavy favorites — expect Spain to control the tempo and look for openings through the flanks.
39: **Belgium**'s golden generation may be fading, but there's still plenty of quality to trouble a **IR Iran** side built on defensive organization and discipline. The Iranians will look to absorb pressure and hit on the counter, making this a potentially frustrating afternoon for the Red Devils — but Belgium's attacking firepower should ultimately prove the difference.
37: **Uruguay** will be heavy favorites to take care of business against **Cabo Verde**, a lively but limited Group H opponent. La Celeste's battle-hardened defense and experienced attacking unit give them every tool to control this contest, though the Cape Verdeans are well-drilled and capable of making life awkward — expect Uruguay to grind it out rather than dazzle.
40: **New Zealand** and **Egypt** meet in a Group G clash that has the feel of a genuine must-win for both sides. The All Whites bring energy and a never-say-die spirit, while the Pharaohs carry genuine technical quality and set-piece danger — it's tight on paper, and a draw may ultimately suit neither, making this a compelling late fixture to close out the day.
34: **Ecuador** are strong favorites against tournament debutants **Curaçao**, the smallest nation ever to reach this stage. La Tri's miserly defense was among the stingiest in qualifying, and they'll look to control the game and ease toward the knockouts — while **Curaçao**, with nothing to lose, will sit deep and hunt a famous counter-attacking moment.
36: **Japan**, one of Asia's most polished and well-organized sides, meet a physical **Tunisia** team that thrives on set pieces and defensive discipline. The Samurai Blue's quick, technical passing will test the Carthage Eagles, who'll look to slow the tempo and strike on the break — and after **Netherlands**' five-goal hammering of **Sweden** earlier today, both sides know exactly what a loss here could cost them in Group F.
